title: Daily Market Digest: Geopolitical Tensions & Tech Resilience
subtitle: July 9, 2026 - Market Pulse

items.headline: Geopolitical Tensions Drive Market Hesitation, Tech Shows Resilience
items.summary: Global markets are navigating a landscape of fragile stability and hesitation on July 9, 2026, as renewed U.S.-Iran conflict elevates crude oil prices and revives inflation concerns. The market narrative has shifted from a soft-landing expectation to an 'oil-shock trade' in less than a week, with AI-related gains struggling against rising crude, rate expectations, and geopolitical risk. Despite these headwinds, U.S. stock futures edged higher ahead of Thursday's session, indicating some investor resilience, particularly in the tech sector. The bond market, however, continues to reflect unease, with government bonds selling off and the 2-year Treasury yield nearing its highest level of the year.

items.headline: Penguin Solutions Inc. Soars on Strong Earnings
items.summary: Penguin Solutions Inc. ($PENG) shares soared 25.1% after reporting third-quarter fiscal 2026 adjusted earnings of $0.84 per share, surpassing the Zacks Consensus Estimate of $0.63 per share.

items.headline: Enerpac Tool Group Jumps on Earnings Beat
items.summary: Enerpac Tool Group Corp. ($EPAC) jumped 6.3% after posting third-quarter fiscal 2026 adjusted earnings of $0.60 per share, beating the Zacks Consensus Estimate of $0.49 per share.

items.headline: NVIDIA Surges on Potential China Chip Deal
items.summary: NVIDIA Corp. ($NVDA) shares surged 3.7% following news that China may allow its major AI companies to purchase a limited number of H200 chips.

items.headline: Broadcom Gains on Apple Partnership
items.summary: U.S. chipmaker Broadcom surged 4.8% after Apple committed to a multiyear partnership with the company.

items.headline: Carnival Corporation Tumbles Amid Rising Oil Prices
items.summary: Shares of Carnival Corporation Ltd. ($CCL) tumbled 3.9% following a spike in crude oil prices due to the escalation of the Middle East war.

items.headline: Sector Performance (July 8): Energy and Healthcare Lead, Tech and Industrials Lag
items.summary: On Wednesday, July 8, the Energy Select Sector SPDR (XLE) led gains with a 2.8% increase, followed by the Health Care Select Sector SPDR (XLV) up 1.5% and Real Estate Select Sector SPDR (XLRE) up 1.4%. Conversely, the Information Technology Select Sector SPDR (XLK) fell 2.4%, and the Industrials Select Sector SPDR (XLI) declined by 1.7%.

items.headline: Investors Await Jobless Claims and Existing Home Sales Data
items.summary: Investors are keenly awaiting the release of Thursday's weekly jobless claims report and existing home sales data for fresh clues on the health of the U.S. economy.

items.headline: Fed Minutes Signal Continued Tightening Possibility Amid Rate Division
items.summary: Minutes from the Federal Reserve's June meeting revealed policymakers remain divided on the path for interest rates, reinforcing expectations that rates could stay higher for longer unless inflation shows clearer signs of easing. The minutes were hawkish enough to keep further tightening on the table. The CME Group's FedWatch tool currently projects a 72.7% likelihood of the Federal Reserve maintaining current interest rates at its July meeting.

items.headline: PepsiCo Reports Q2 Earnings; Simply Good Foods Also Reporting
items.summary: PepsiCo ($PEP) is scheduled to report its quarterly earnings today, July 9, 2026, with Q2 2026 earnings rising on revenue growth. Simply Good Foods ($SMPL) is also slated to release its quarterly results.

items.headline: Covestro Raises 2026 Core Earnings Outlook After Strong H1
items.summary: German chemicals maker Covestro raised its full-year 2026 operating core earnings outlook after reporting stronger-than-expected preliminary results for the first half of 2026, driven by higher selling prices.

items.headline: Middle East Conflict Escalates, Driving Oil Prices and Inflation Concerns
items.summary: The intensified war between the United States and Iran continues to be a primary driver of market sentiment, leading to higher crude oil prices and reviving inflation and rate hike concerns. Brent crude rose 1.2% to $78.94, marking its third consecutive daily gain. However, in early Thursday trading, Brent crude was down 1.89% at $77.47 a barrel, while WTI crude fell 2.02% to $73.36. This geopolitical tension shifts the debate for central banks from temporary volatility to the possibility of a more persistent supply-driven inflation impulse.
